steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the text. The text opens with Hanel's conclusion (reflecting on values makes people more polite), then describes the exact method used: two groups, what each wrote about, and what was observed.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To argue that researchers were surprised by the results of a certain study: No surprise is mentioned anywhere in the text. ✗
  • Choice B — To suggest ways to improve a certain study’s experimental design: The text describes the design neutrally; no improvements are proposed. ✗
  • Choice C — To explain a study’s conclusion and how a research team arrived at that conclusion: The first sentence states the conclusion; the rest walks through the method. Both halves are present. ✓
  • Choice D — To describe a widely held belief and how a study’s results support that belief: No pre-existing belief is mentioned; the study's finding stands on its own. ✗
C is correct.
